Now that you've mastered the art of extracting the two sweetest words in a negotiation, that's right. We're going to practice in a real-life situation. Alright, let's set up the scenario. Currently, you're working as an accountant for a sales branch of an office supply company. You've received a couple of job offers from the corporate office to become a regional accounting director. In order to get this position, you have to get prior approval from your branch's manager. You've been hesitant to ask for approval because this would put you at a pay grade above your current boss. In a previous attempt, it was concluded that your boss wanted you to remain at this branch. Your task is to get your boss to say, that's right, and approve your promotion. You walk into his office. What do you say?
